item.title: Trump says "Americans aren't against" Iran war ahead of dignified transfer of soldiers killed
item.description: President Trump will attend the dignified transfer of the remains of the service members killed in the Middle East. Speaking to reporters on the tarmac, Mr. Trump said that a poll shows that "Americans aren't against the war" with Iran.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha impacting northern Gulf Coast. See its path.
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: How to refinance your student loans now
item.description: Refinancing could lower your payments, but the process — and timing — can play a big role in how much you save.

item.title: FDA recalls antihistamine tablets. Here are the affected products.
item.description: A generic version of the allergy medication Zyrtec is under recall across the U.S. over cross-contamination concerns.

item.title: Luke Bracey talks new "Little House on the Prairie" series and why it resonates with people
item.description: Luke Bracey tells "CBS Mornings" the new "Little House on the Prairie" series was adapted from the books rather than a reboot of the TV show. He explains why he thinks it resonates with people today and how a playlist helped him and a co-star bond over their characters.

item.title: Nolan Wells' cause of death undetermined, family's autopsy shows
item.description: Nolan Wells, 18, was found dead just off Horn Island in Mississippi after enjoying a day out with friends on July 4.

item.title: Trump threatens to bomb a bridge or power plant for every ship Iran attacks
item.description: The Pentagon keeps insisting the Strait of Hormuz is open, but with Iranian attacks continuing, one shipping insider says "nothing is going through."

item.title: Extreme weather hits across the country as Gulf states prepare for Tropical Storm Bertha impact
item.description: Tornadoes swept across parts of the Midwest to the Northeast on Tuesday, leaving behind major damage. Meanwhile, on the Gulf Coast, Tropical Storm Bertha's winds reached 59 mph and rough surf pounded Panama City Beach, Florida. Lana Zak has the latest.

item.title: Anson Mount on why new "Star Trek" season is best so far: "It continues to challenge us"
item.description: Anson Mount previews the fourth season of "Star Trek: Strange New Worlds." He talks about his introduction to "Star Trek" and why he thinks it's the best season yet.

item.title: U.S. reaches deal with Saudis for nuclear energy commercial agreements
item.description: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia are expected to sign a deal for U.S. companies to run nuclear power for the Saudis, potentially allowing the Saudis to enrich their own fuel for nuclear reactors.

item.title: Newsom's office called our diaper contract reporting "demonstrably false." State records show what happened
item.description: Gov. Newsom's office says the no-bid deal was "competitive," the records weren't delayed, and our reporting is false. The state's own database, deadlines, and the Legislature's budget bills say otherwise. Here's the point-by-point review.

item.title: Trump-endorsed candidates projected to win big in Arizona GOP primaries
item.description: Republican candidates endorsed by President Trump will win key races in Arizona's primary elections, the Associated Press projects. CBS News' Fin Gómez reports.

item.title: Democrats probe DOJ opinion that eases institutionalization of mentally ill
item.description: Democrats in the Senate and House are investigating the origins of a DOJ legal opinion that would make it easier for states to keep people with mental illness in institutions, rather than helping them to live at home.

item.title: Behind Norah O'Donnell's new podcast "Healthful"
item.description: Norah O'Donnell talks about her new podcast, "Healthful with Norah O'Donnell," which is about providing helpful and trusted information on women's health. Each week, she speaks with leading experts on everything from mental health to fitness.

item.title: The health and environmental benefits of sugar kelp | 60 Minutes Archive
item.description: From 2018, Lesley Stahl explores ocean farming, and reports on the health and environmental benefits of seaweed.

item.title: Zelenskyy fires commander-in-chief after protests amid cabinet turmoil
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired his commander-in-chief and named a replacement after days of protests. This came as Ukraine targeted an online retailer that it says facilitates Russian military logistics. CBS News' Aidan Stretch reports.

item.title: France becomes first EU country to ban social media for kids under 15
item.description: As lawmakers approve a social media ban on kids and phones in high schools, Macron says France is "leading the way in Europe when it comes to protecting our children."

item.title: New Jersey investigating election software glitch that added 6,600 noncitizens to voter rolls
item.description: New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill announced an investigation into a software error that accidentally registered 6,600 noncitizens to vote. CBS News election law contributor David Becker weighs in.

item.title: Which credit card hardship programs are available now and how can you qualify?
item.description: Credit card relief options can vary widely. If you're struggling, here's what to know before you ask for help.

item.title: U.S. tech executives sounding the alarm on Chinese AI models, WSJ reports
item.description: U.S. tech giants are concerned about cheap Chinese AI models, according to a new report from The Wall Street Journal. Amrith Ramkumar, a tech policy reporter at The Wall Street Journal who contributed to the story, joins to discuss.

item.title: Exclusive discounts from CBS Mornings Deals
item.description: On this edition of CBS Mornings Deals, we show you items that will help improve your everyday lifestyle. Visit cbsdeals.com to take advantage of these exclusive deals today. CBS earns commissions on purchases made through cbsdeals.com.

item.title: Trump approves nuclear deal that could allow Saudi Arabia to enrich uranium, AP reports
item.description: President Trump approved a nuclear deal that could allow Saudi Arabia to enrich uranium, the Associated Press reports. CBS News' Courtney Kealy has more details.

item.title: Lung cancer in nonsmokers: "A women's health crisis"
item.description: Lung cancer is the leading cause of cancer death in American women — and a growing share of new cases are in women who've never smoked.

item.title: Nearly a third of Americans say they've shoplifted, survey finds
item.description: Nearly a third of Americans say they have resorted to shoplifting, a new survey shows. CBS News MoneyWatch reporter Megan Cerullo has tips for managing rising costs.

item.title: Tenisha Warner, widow of Malcolm-Jamal Warner, opens up about being a single mom
item.description: Tenisha Warner sat down for an exclusive interview with "CBS Mornings" before a lawsuit over her late husband's estate became public, and discussed her grief and raising her daughter alone.

item.title: Software error improperly registered 6,600 noncitizens to vote, N.J. gov. says
item.description: New Jersey Gov. Mikie Sherrill said a preliminary analysis showed that fewer than 400 of the people registered that way went on to cast ballots.

item.title: OpenAI says its technology, on its own, hacked another AI company
item.description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI says its artificial intelligence system hacked into another AI company on its own in what the company called an "unprecedented cyber incident."

item.title: ICE detentions hit highest monthly total in June since crackdown began
item.description: More than 43,000 immigrants were arrested last month as DHS continues to detain people in less-publicized enforcement surges.

item.title: Mamdani says he won't try to arrest Israel's Netanyahu
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani conceded late Tuesday that the city does not have the authority to arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

item.title: Australian woman gives birth to rare identical quadruplets
item.description: All four daughters share 100 percent of their DNA after a single fertilized egg split into four embryos, making the pregnancy a one-in-15-million occurrence.
